topStories.title: Wildfires Race Across US as Drought Spans Half the Nation

topStories.title: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, dies week after he was injured in US-Israeli strikes
topStories.description: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, died from injuries sustained in US-Israeli airstrikes on April 1. He was 81 and head of the Strategic Council for International Relations. He previously represented Iran at the UN in New York and later served as foreign minister from 1997 to 2005

topStories.title: Pakistan preps for make-or-break Washington-Tehran meeting - but will Iran show up? All you need to know
topStories.description: Pakistan is preparing to host talks between US and Iranian delegations on Saturday, though Iran's participation is uncertain due to recent Israeli strikes in Lebanon. The agenda includes Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, with discussions likely to be indirect.

dailyNews.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

dailyNews.title: US has let in 4,499 refugees since October - all but three were South African
dailyNews.description: Trump has given priority to Afrikaners, a white minority group he says is being persecuted. South Africa has objected to his characterisation.

dailyNews.title: US waiver on Russian oil sanctions expires on 11 April — Will US extend the relaxations amid talks with Iran?
dailyNews.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin's special envoy Kirill Dmitriev is currently in the US, meeting US President Donald Trump's administration for discussions on a peace deal with Ukraine. It was not clear whether the talks will extend to the sanctions.

dailyNews.title: Iran ceasefire: Not an off-ramp for the US but a life-saving ejection seat
dailyNews.description: A Middle Eastern country has for the first time single-handedly checked the war-making capabilities of the US and Israel

dailyNews.title: Bessent, Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic's new ‘Mythos’ AI model — What risks did they flag?
dailyNews.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell met with bank CEOs to discuss cybersecurity risks from Anthropic's Mythos AI model.

dailyNews.title: Former Baylor women's basketball star Melissa Jones dead at 36
dailyNews.description: Former Baylor women's basketball star Melissa Jones, one of only seven players to have her jersey retired by the program, has died at age 36.

dailyNews.title: Kim Kardashian’s $6M deal with Ray J explodes as judge refuses to keep secrets sealed
dailyNews.description: A California judge denied Kim Kardashian's request to seal her $6 million sex tape settlement with Ray J, potentially exposing the 2023 agreement.

dailyNews.title: Cold War bunker 'waiting for Armageddon' found beneath medieval castle in 'perfect location'
dailyNews.description: Experts uncovered a lost Cold War nuclear bunker on the grounds of Scarborough Castle, a medieval fortress in North Yorkshire, England. The hideout had been presumed lost.

dailyNews.title: Artemis II crew closes in on Earth as mission ends with Pacific splashdown and more top headlines
dailyNews.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

dailyNews.title: Al Sharpton says America's 250th anniversary is not a 'celebration' for Black people, calls it 'crazy'
dailyNews.description: Rev. Al Sharpton claimed it's "crazy" to expect Black people to celebrate America's upcoming 250th anniversary, noting they were enslaved when it was founded.

dailyNews.title: 'To Catch a Predator' host Chris Hansen warns Roblox dangers far worse than past online chatrooms
dailyNews.description: Chris Hansen compared Roblox to an amusement park where kids are flying off rides, warning parents about what he claims is a surge of grooming.

topStories.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed despite a ceasefire deal, with 800 tankers among 3,200 vessels backed up and mariners stranded.

topStories.description: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Changes Lock Some Employers Out of H-1B Visa Program
dailyNews.description: Since imposing a $100,000 fee on new visas in September, the Trump administration has upended the skilled worker program.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Returns to Court for Yet Another Tariff Lawsuit
dailyNews.description: States and small businesses challenged the 10 percent tax on many imports that President Trump imposed after the Supreme Court struck down a previous slate of tariffs.

dailyNews.title: Where Mail Voting Began, Worries Spread Over Trump’s Attacks
dailyNews.description: In the Pacific Northwest, mail-in ballots have been the norm for decades, but President Trump’s war on such voting has turned a point of regional pride into another partisan battle line.

dailyNews.title: Trump warns Iran against charging tolls to go through Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Only about a dozen ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the first two days of the ceasefire, far below normal traffic levels before the war, data shows.

dailyNews.title: 4/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: President Trump says he asked Netanyahu to scale back Lebanon strikes; Melania Trump makes a rare statement denying relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

dailyNews.title: Trump criticizes NATO after talks with Rutte
dailyNews.description: President Trump bashed NATO after meeting with Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday. Ken Thomas, national politics reporter for the Wall Street Journal and Dave Levinthal, senior editor for NOTUS, joined "The Takeout" to discuss that and some of the day's other top political headlines.

dailyNews.title: White House staff received email warning not to place bets on prediction markets
dailyNews.description: White House aides got an email last month telling them not to place bets on prediction markets with nonpublic information, multiple administration officials told CBS News.

dailyNews.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes Further Threatening Ceasefire
dailyNews.description: New Israeli strikes in Lebanon targeting what it says is the Iran-backed militia Hezbollah are threatening the fragile ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran. Leaders of Iran say Lebanon is an inseparable part of the ceasefire, which the U.S. and Israel dispute. It comes as the White House team is set to meet Iranian officials in Pakistan for talks this weekend. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY from Tel Aviv.

dailyNews.title: A mother mourning the son she lost in the Iran war says she told Trump: 'Go get 'em'
dailyNews.description: More than a month after her son, Maj. Cody Khork, died in the Iran war, Donna Burhans has found a routine that helps her cope.

dailyNews.title: Exclusive: Cuban president reacts to Trump admin’s call for him to step down
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says "stepping down [is] not part of our vocabulary" amid reports that the U.S. government has asked the Cuban leader to resign.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.
